name: Farmington Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Farmington Community Center in Farmington, ME! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include restrooms.

name: Hippach Field
description: Hippach Field is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Farmington, ME. There are 2 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Kineowatha Park
description: Come play pickleball at Kineowatha Park in Wilton, ME! There are 2 outdoor courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Pickleball Rangeley
description: Come play pickleball at Pickleball Rangeley in Rangeley, ME! There are 3 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, and a pro shop or equipment store.

name: Rangeley Fitness Center
description: Come play pickleball at Rangeley Fitness Center in Rangeley, ME! There is 1 indoor court. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Santosha at Hillholm Estate
description: We've recently refurbished the early-'70s clay (Har-Tru) tennis court on our grounds and have added pickleball lines. The court had not been used for nearly a decade, and it is going to take some time to bring it back to pristine condition, but it's getting close and pickleball on clay is super fun! Clay requires a different ball than hard courts. We have paddles and clay balls to sign out. If players would like to bring their own pickleballs, we recommend Onix Pure 2 outdoor balls.
open_play_schedule: Community members can schedule one-hour blocks of court time

name: Witon Academy School
description: Witon Academy School is a place to play pickleball in Wilton, ME. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. Amenities include water. The courts are free.
